pages.title: About - Yasmin Qureshi
pages.meta_description: Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden
headings: h3: Contact details
h3: Further details
pages.text_excerpt: About - Yasmin Qureshi Skip to main content Yasmin Qureshi Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden Join Get Involved Donate Homepage News Articles and Press Parliamentary Activity About Contact Me Useful Contacts Privacy Policy Acceptable Behaviour Policy Help and Advice Immigration Casework and Information More Search Search the website Search Menu About Photograph of Yasmin Qureshi I am the Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden, elected in 2024 following the electoral boundary changes. I was previously the Member of Parliament (MP) for Bolton South East, first elected in the May 2010 general election, and re-elected in 2015 and 2017. In 2007, I was selected to be the Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for Bolton South East from an open shortlist. I left Chambers in London and moved to Bolton and practised law from Kenworthy Chambers, Manchester. At the General Election on 6 May 2010, I won with a majority of 8,634. Parliamentary Career I was a member of the Justice Select Committee I was the Shadow Minister for Women and Equalities . I was the Shadow Minister for International Development from April 2020 to December 2021. I was the Shadow Minister for Justice from October 2016 until April 2020. My responsibilities in this role included: Court Services and reform Parliamentary matters arising from the Queen’s Speech (incl. Whiplash and Draft Domestic Violence & Abuse Bill) Victims (including domestic abuse) Supporting the Secretary of State for Justice on EU Exit & international business Criminal justice Human rights Family justice Admin, tribunal and immigration Coroners, burials, inquests & inquiries Lammy Review & Race Disparity Audit Law Commission Mental capacity and office of the Public Guardian Select Committees Prior to becoming Shadow Justice Minister, I sat on the following Select Committees: Foreign Affairs Sub-Committee Jan 2016 – Oct 2016 Foreign Affairs Committee Jul 2015 – Oct 2016 High Speed Rail (London – West Midlands) Bill (Commons) Apr 2014 – Jul 2015 Home Affairs Committee Nov 2013 – Mar 2015 Privacy and Injunctions (Joint Committee) Jul 2011 – Mar 2012 Political and Constitutional Reform Committee Jul 2011 – Dec 2011 Justice Committee Jul 2010 – Mar 2015 All-Party Parliamentary Groups (APPGs) I am an officer of the following APPGs: Britain-Pakistan Trade and Tourism Childcare and Early Education Dalits Dentistry and Oral Health Dyslexia and Other Specific Learning Difficulties First Do No Harm Greater Manchester Hajj and Umrah Hormone Pregnancy Tests Inclusive Entrepreneurship Pakistan Religion in the Media Srebrenica Taxis Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus I am also an ordinary member of the following APPGs: Equitable Life Policyholders Fracking Haemophilia and Contaminated Blood Leasehold and Commonhold Reform Left Behind’ Neighbourhoods Prior to becoming MP for Bolton South East: Education I obtained a BA (Hons) Law degree and completed my Barrister at Laws examinations at the Council of Legal Education. I obtained a Master of Laws at University College London. Career I began my legal career with the Crown Prosecution Service as an in-house Barrister. I prosecuted on behalf of the Crown in numerous criminal cases. Later, I became a specialist casework advocate dealing with complex and sensitive cases. (i) United Nations Mission in Kosovo (UNMIK) I was the co-ordinator of the Criminal Legal Unit from 2000-2001. This involved addressing the obstacles and problems, in matters of law and practical procedure, which hindered the effective investigation and prosecution of criminal offences. To ensure these methods were successful my team of lawyers and I put into place various mechanisms: such as a duty rota of Gynaecologists who would assess rape victims, team of forensics, pathologists and doctors to look at Murder cases. I set up a training programme to support teams of international police officers who arrived in Kosovo to support the region, to ensure that they wer

pages.text_excerpt: Contact Me - Yasmin Qureshi Skip to main content Yasmin Qureshi Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden Join Get Involved Donate Homepage News Articles and Press Parliamentary Activity About Contact Me Useful Contacts Privacy Policy Acceptable Behaviour Policy Help and Advice Immigration Casework and Information More Search Search the website Search Menu Contact Me Yasmin Qureshi Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden Address Please send all letters to: Yasmin Qureshi MP House of Commons LONDON SW1A 0AA Packages from courier companies should addressed to Yasmin Qureshi MP c/o CEVA Logistics, 40-48 Chase Road, London, NW10 6PX. . Please do not send post directly to the constituency office as it will take at least two days longer to reach me. . Please do not come to my constituency office in person without an appointment; my team is not able to see people who have not booked an appointment in advance. Please send all letters to: Yasmin Qureshi MP House of Commons LONDON SW1A 0AA Packages from courier companies should addressed to Yasmin Qureshi MP c/o CEVA Logistics, 40-48 Chase Road, London, NW10 6PX. . Please do not send post directly to the constituency office as it will take at least two days longer to reach me. . Please do not come to my constituency office in person without an appointment; my team is not able to see people who have not booked an appointment in advance. '> View on Google Maps Telephone 01204 371202 Email qureshiy2@parliament.uk If you want to contact me about any issue, please include your full name and full postal address in your letter or email. If you have casework or issues, the best way to contact me is via the webform here: https://members.parliament.uk/member/3924/contact The constituency office telephone number is 01204 371202 . Please do not come to my constituency office in person without an appointment – my team and I are not able to see people who do not have an appointment booked in advance. I am able to help constituents with the following issues: Tax problems involving the HM Revenue and Customs Department Welfare issues dealt with by the Department for Work and Pensions such as benefits, tax credits and state pensions Issues handled by the Home Office such as immigration and passports (but I cannot give immigration advice, see below) Issues relating to the Department of Health and Social Care such as concerns about hospitals or access to treatment Any issues arising from any other government departments Issues with utility companies Consumer issues I can support constituents by: Writing to the Minister with responsibility for the department concerned Asking an official for an explanation of why a decision has been made Asking for a decision to be reviewed What I can’t help with: Please note that I am not legally permitted to give immigration advice. For all immigration advice, you should speak to a qualified legal adviser. I am not able to countersign photographs for passports or driving licences. I am not able to provide references for people whom I have not known well for at least two years. My office is not presently able to offer work experience, shadowing or internships. If you wish to report an immigration crime, such as someone living or working in the UK illegally, employing someone who isn’t allowed to work in the UK, involved in smuggling or involved in illegal immigration, you must fill in the online form here: https://www.gov.uk/report-immigration-crime Asking an MP to report it on your behalf will only slow down the progress of the report and create unnecessary duplication. Please see the advice section of my website for further information on immigration casework . Early Day Motions (EDMs) I don’t usually sign EDMs. Whilst they may bring an issue to public attention, they rarely have a meaningful effect and there are numerous other ways of effectively raising an issue. Signing EDMs requires virtually no effort and it has become an easy alterna

pages.title: Immigration Casework and Information - Yasmin Qureshi
pages.meta_description: Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden
headings: h3: Contact details
h3: Further details
pages.text_excerpt: Immigration Casework and Information - Yasmin Qureshi Skip to main content Yasmin Qureshi Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden Join Get Involved Donate Homepage News Articles and Press Parliamentary Activity About Contact Me Useful Contacts Privacy Policy Acceptable Behaviour Policy Help and Advice Immigration Casework and Information More Search Search the website Search Menu Immigration Casework and Information Asking your MP for assistance Please note that Members of Parliament and their staff are not legally permitted to give immigration advice. If you need immigration advice, you should contact a suitably qualified and insured immigration adviser. You can find advisers here: https://portal.oisc.gov.uk/s/adviser-finder Please could you provide all of the details below in order for Ms. Qureshi to contact the Home Office regarding your immigration matter: Full Name Full Address with Post Code Date of Birth Application Type Application Reference Number/ Home Office reference number Date that the application was submitted Please could you also go into your online account and take a screenshot of the page showing the application reference number and the date the application was made, and then send that screenshot to us. Asking your MP to chase up the progress on an application Unless your application is very urgent, please do not ask the MP to chase up an application which is still within the service standards periods for the relevant type of application: Visa processing times for applications made inside the UK Visa processing times for application made outside the UK Please note that applications for Further Leave to Remain on the Basis of Family and Private Life (FLTR-FP) do not have a service standard. Please do not ask the MP to chase any FLTR-FP applications unless your circumstances have become urgent. Consent to Act We will not be able to take any action without the signed consent of the applicant. Therefore, we will require a ‘wet signature’ signed consent form from the applicant – i.e. signed with a pen, not just typed. The applicant should complete and sign this form: UKVI – Consent for the MP to Act . Please return the form to qureshiy2@parliament.uk as soon as possible A scan or photograph of the complete form will be acceptable. Once we have received this information and consent form, we will look into your issue. Self-Help Right to Remain is an organisation which provides lots of guidance and assistance for those applying for the right to remain in the UK. have an excellent free-to-use Toolkit which provide a step-by-step guide to the asylum and immigration system, helping applicants to understand their and navigate the system. The information provided comes not only legal professionals but also from people have been through the system themselves and understand the difficulties and emotional aspects of the process. Migrant Help provides independent advice and guidance to assist people seeking asylum in the UK, including applying for accommodation and financial support. They may also provide specialist support and accommodation to victims of human trafficking and modern slavery, and their dependants. The Home Office public enquiries email address has now been discontinued. If you want to contact the Home Office directly, please go here: https://www.gov.uk/guidance/contact-the-home-office Keeping your details up-to-date with the Home Office Visa Applicants It is essential that you notify the Home Office of any changes in your details, including: passport/travel documents home address or postal address phone number email address change of legal representative Failure to do so could lead to delays in processing your application. Your MP cannot update your details for you; you must do this yourself by going to this web page: https://visa-address-update.service.gov.uk/ Asylum Seekers If you are an asylum seeker, please email your updated details to asylumcentralcommunicationshub@homeoffice.gov.uk As
